questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: He asked that whether I could help him.
options: He asked that whether I could help him.
He asked if whether I could help him.
He asked that I could help him.
He asked whether I could help him.
questions.solution: He asked whether I could help him.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: The commitee (A) / has given (B) / their report (C) / on time. (D)

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: The committee (A) / have submitted (B) / its report (C) / to the board. (D)

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: She was angry on her friend for lying.
options: She was angry to her friend for lying.
She was angry on her friend for lying.
She was angry with her friend for lying.
She was angry at her friend for lying.
questions.solution: She was angry with her friend for lying.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: Neither he (A) / nor his brother (B) / are going (C) / to the party. (D)

questions.stem: Correct: He told the news to his brother and I.
options: He told the news to I and my brother.
He told the news to his brother and me.
He told the news to his brother and I.
He told the news to my brother and myself.
questions.solution: He told the news to his brother and me.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Digital multimeter: ADC type typically used:
questions.solution: Dual-slope: accurate, noise-rejected, slow. Good for DMM where speed isn't critical but accuracy is.

questions.stem: An LC tank has L = 1 µH and C = 10 pF. Its resonant frequency is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Logic families — CMOS:
options: Low power (static). Complementary p/n MOS. V_DD 3-15V

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A continuous LTI system is stable if all poles of its transfer function lie in the:
questions.solution: Poles with negative real parts (left half-plane) give decaying, stable responses.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fourier Transform of unit impulse δ(t):

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Friis transmission formula: P_r = P_t·G_t·G_r·(λ/(4πR))². Doubling distance R:
options: P_r decreases by factor of 4 (-6 dB)
questions.solution: P_r ∝ 1/R². Double R → P_r decreases by 4×.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An n-channel enhancement MOSFET enters the saturation (active) region when:
options: V_GS > V_th and V_DS ≥ V_GS − V_th
questions.solution: Above threshold and with V_DS pinching off the channel, the MOSFET saturates and acts as a current source.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: MOSFET: subthreshold conduction occurs when:
options: Gate voltage is below threshold but above zero (weak inversion)
questions.solution: Subthreshold: weak inversion. Current increases exponentially. Important for low-power design.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: MOV instruction in 8085:
options: Copies data from source to destination (reg/reg or reg/mem)
questions.solution: MOV Rd, Rs or MOV Rd, M. Memory addressing: HL register pair points to memory.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Block diagram reduction: parallel blocks combine by:
questions.solution: Parallel: Y = G1*X + G2*X = (G1+G2)*X. Series: multiply.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: 8255 PPI port A can operate in:
questions.solution: 8255: Port A: 3 modes. Port B: Mode 0,1. Port C: bit-set/reset or handshake signals.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Sliding mode control: sliding surface designed for:
questions.solution: Sliding surface s=0 defines reduced-order dynamics. Control forces state to surface, then slides along it. Robust to matched uncertainties.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The ALU in a CPU performs:
questions.solution: The Arithmetic Logic Unit executes add/subtract and AND/OR/NOT-type operations.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Clegg integrator: nonlinear integrator that resets when:
questions.solution: Clegg: resets state to zero when output crosses zero. Effective phase lag less than linear integrator. Improves transient response.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The 8086 microprocessor has:
options: 16-bit data bus, 20-bit address bus (1 MB memory)

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Poles of a transfer function are:
options: Roots of denominator (natural frequencies of system)
questions.solution: Poles: values of s where H(s) → ∞. System stable if all poles have Re(s) < 0.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The AM waveform's envelope (shown) has Amax = 15 V and Amin = 6 V. The modulation index is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The energy bandgap of Si at 300K is:
questions.solution: Si: 1.12 eV (indirect). Ge: 0.67 eV. GaAs: 1.43 eV (direct). Wider gap = higher temp capability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about an LTI system's impulse response h(t) is correct?
options: h(t) determines only the DC gain
Convolution with h(t) is irrelevant to the output
The system is causal if h(t) = 0 for t < 0
questions.solution: A causal system cannot respond before the input, so h(t) = 0 for t < 0.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: PCM SNR for 14-bit quantization:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about a Zener diode regulator is correct?
options: Its voltage rises steeply with current in breakdown
It must be reverse biased below breakdown
It maintains a nearly constant voltage in reverse breakdown
questions.solution: In reverse breakdown the Zener voltage stays almost constant over a range of current.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: According to the sampling theorem, a signal of maximum frequency fm must be sampled at a rate of at least:
questions.solution: The Nyquist rate is 2fm; sampling slower causes aliasing.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Spread spectrum processing gain:
options: Bandwidth of spread signal / data bandwidth
questions.solution: PG = BWspread / BWdata. Provides interference rejection. Higher PG → more resistant to jamming/narrowband interference.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Direct bandgap semiconductor suitable for LEDs:
questions.solution: Direct gap: electron-hole recombination emits photon. Si, Ge are indirect → poor light emitters.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Fourier transform of x(t) = cos(ω₀t) is:
questions.solution: F{cos(ω₀t)} = π[δ(ω-ω₀) + δ(ω+ω₀)]. F{sin(ω₀t)} = jπ[δ(ω+ω₀) - δ(ω-ω₀)].

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: State-space: controllability matrix C = [B AB A^2B ... A^(n-1)B]. System is controllable if:
questions.solution: Controllability: can all states be driven to desired values? Full rank = yes. Kalman test.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Laplace transform is especially useful for analysing:
options: Transient and stability behaviour of systems
questions.solution: The s-domain handles initial conditions, transients and pole locations for stability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An inverting op-amp has Rf = 100 kΩ and Rin = 10 kΩ. Its voltage gain is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Second-order system: ω_n=20 rad/s, ζ=0.5. Percentage overshoot ≈

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Op-amp — input bias current:
options: DC current into input terminals. I_b = (I_+ + I_-)/2. For BJT: μA. For FET: pA
questions.solution: I_b: causes offset voltage due to source resistance. Compensate with equal R on both inputs.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Phase margin is:
questions.solution: PM = 180° + ∠GH(jω_gc). PM > 30-60° for good transient response.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Steady-state error for type-1 system with step input:
questions.solution: Type-1: pole at origin. Step → e_ss = 0. Ramp → e_ss = 1/K_v.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sensitivity of closed-loop:
options: S = 1/(1+G(s)H(s)). Less sensitive to plant variations than open-loop
questions.solution: Sensitivity: small at low f (|GH| large). Increases near crossover. Trade-off with stability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Non-inverting: Rf=220kΩ, R₁=10kΩ, Vin=5V. Vout:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Lead-lag compensator:
options: Lead section: transient. Lag section: steady-state. Combined design
questions.solution: Lead-lag: cascade. Design lead first for PM, then lag for SSE.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Moore state machine output depends on:
questions.solution: Moore: output associated with state. One clock delay from input to output.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An 3-bit ADC provides how many quantization levels?

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Carry lookahead adder advantage:
questions.solution: CLA: computes carry bits simultaneously using generate/propagate. Reduces delay from O(n) to O(1).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: K-map for 3-variable function: f=Σm(1,3,5,7). Minimized:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A decoder converts:
options: n inputs to 2^n outputs (one active at a time)
questions.solution: Decoder: n → 2^n. 3-to-8 decoder (74138): 3 inputs → 8 outputs, active-low.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Static Timing Analysis (STA):
options: Validate setup and hold times. Critical path analysis. No test vectors needed
questions.solution: STA: worst-case timing. Clock skew analysis. Setup: data arrival < clock + T_cycle - T_setup. Hold: data > clock + T_hold.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the logic circuit shown, Y = (A AND B) OR C with A = 0, B = 0, C = 1. The output Y is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: CMOS logic gate consumes power primarily during:
questions.solution: CMOS: near-zero static power. Dynamic power = C·V²·f·α (switching).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Boolean identity: A+AB=

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Serial communication — UART:
options: Asynchronous. Start bit, data (5-9 bits), parity, stop bit. Baud rate
questions.solution: UART: universal async receiver-transmitter. RS-232, RS-485. 8N1, 8E1, 8O1 format.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fan-out:
options: Maximum number of inputs a gate can drive. TTL: 10. CMOS: 50+
questions.solution: Fan-out: I_OH(total) ≤ I_OH(max). I_OL(total) ≤ I_OL(max). CMOS: capacitive fan-out.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Logic families — TTL:
questions.solution: TTL: multi-emitter input. Totem-pole output. 74LS (low-power Schottky): popular.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: 2-bit ripple counter counts from:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Serial communication — SPI:
options: Synchronous. Master/slave. MOSI, MISO, SCLK, SS. Full duplex. No addressing
questions.solution: SPI: 4 wires. Multiple slaves with individual SS. Faster than I2C. No acknowledge.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cascode amplifier:
options: CE-CB (BJT) or CS-CG (FET). High gain, high bandwidth, low Miller effect
questions.solution: Cascode: CB stage shields CE from Miller effect. Av ≈ -gm·Rc. High f_T.

questions.stem: Oscillation condition (Barkhausen):
questions.solution: Start: |Aβ| > 1. Steady state: |Aβ| = 1. Phase shift: 0° or 360°.

questions.stem: Probability of error in ASK: P_e = 0.5·erfc(√(E_b/(2N₀))). Compared to BPSK:
questions.solution: ASK needs 3 dB more power than BPSK for same BER. Coherent detection assumed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Transformer coupled amplifier:
options: Provides impedance matching and DC isolation
questions.solution: Transformer: turn ratio N₁/N₂ for impedance matching (Z_in/Z_out = (N₁/N₂)²).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Class A amplifier:
options: Conduction angle 360°. Low efficiency (<25% for RC coupled, <50% for transformer coupled)
questions.solution: Class A: always on. Low distortion, low efficiency. Used in small-signal stages.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The magnitude response shown is flat then rolls off at −20 dB/decade past f_c ≈ 1000 Hz. This is the response of a:
questions.solution: Constant gain at low frequencies with a single −20 dB/decade roll-off above f_c ≈ 1000 Hz is a first-order low-pass response.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An ideal op-amp has:
options: Infinite input impedance, zero output impedance, infinite gain
questions.solution: Ideal op-amp: Z_in = ∞, Z_out = 0, A_OL = ∞, BW = ∞.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: DMA (Direct Memory Access):
options: Peripheral ↔ memory without CPU. DMA controller. Cycle stealing or burst mode
questions.solution: DMA: 1+ channel. Transfer types: single, block, demand. Scatter-gather for physical memory.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The built-in potential of a silicon diode is:
questions.solution: Si: ~0.7 V. Ge: ~0.3 V. GaAs: ~1.2 V. V_bi = (kT/q)·ln(N_A N_D/n_i²).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Modulation — need for:
options: Frequency translation, antenna size (λ/4), multiplexing, noise immunity
questions.solution: Modulation: shift baseband to carrier frequency. Practical antenna size ∝ λ.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Negative resistance oscillator: oscillation condition:
questions.solution: Barkhausen criterion: loop gain = 1 with 0° phase. Negative resistance cancels tank loss for sustained oscillation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A high common-mode rejection ratio (CMRR) in a differential amplifier means it:
options: Strongly rejects signals common to both inputs
questions.solution: CMRR = differential gain/common-mode gain; high CMRR suppresses noise common to both inputs.

questions.stem: Routh-Hurwitz: s⁴+1s³+4s²+5s+2=0. System is:
questions.solution: All coefficients positive and Routh array checks needed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ethernet:
options: CSMA/CD (legacy). Switched now. MAC address. OSI Layer 1 (PHY) + Layer 2 (MAC)
questions.solution: Ethernet: 10/100/1000 Mbps. RJ-45 connector. Auto-negotiation. Full duplex (switched).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Quarter-wave transformer matches Z_L to Z₀ using impedance:
questions.solution: λ/4: Z_in = Z_T²/Z_L. For Z_in=Z₀: Z_T = √(Z₀Z_L).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Registers in 8085:
options: A (accumulator), B, C, D, E, H, L (general purpose), SP, PC, flags
questions.solution: 8085 registers: 8-bit general (B,C,D,E,H,L) + 16-bit (SP, PC). Flags: S, Z, AC, P, CY.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cellular — handoff (handover):
options: Transfer call between cells. Hard handoff (break-before-make). Soft handoff (CDMA)
questions.solution: Handoff: signal strength + quality. Mobile-assisted handoff (MAHO). Handover margin.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: 8086 segmented memory: segment registers CS, DS, SS, ES:
options: CS: code, DS: data, SS: stack, ES: extra data
questions.solution: Segment registers: CS (IP), DS (data), SS (SP/BP), ES (string operations, extra data).
